channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Streaming Dilemma: When Does the Cost Stop Going Up?
item.description: The cost of streaming TV services continues to rise. Is it time to take a break and explore free alternatives? Discover a world of no-cost options, from Pluto TV to over-the-air TV, that can scratch your entertainment itch on the cheap.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: AI use cases are going to get even bigger in 2025
item.description: Over the past two years, generative AI has dominated tech conversations and media headlines. Tools like ChatGPT, Gemini, Midjourney, and Sora captured imaginations with their ability to create text, images, and videos, sparking both excitement and ethical debates.
item.dc:creator: Kolawole Samuel Adebayo, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk’s preschool is the next step in his anti-woke education dreams
item.description: Alongside rockets, electric cars, brain implants, social media and the next Trump administration, the world's richest person is increasingly focused on education, spanning preschool to college.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Timeshare terror leaves retired couple $50K in the hole after being scammed
item.description: The FBI is warning timeshare owners of a telemarketing scam tied to a Mexican drug cartel. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides steps to stay safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Opinion: Don’t cloak artificial intelligence in national security secrecy
item.description: Biden administration directive could make an already covert system even more unaccountable and opaque.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Guariglia, The Mercury News

item.title: Tech supply chains at risk as the US launches probe into China’s legacy chip dominance
item.description: The US has intensified its campaign to counter China’s ambitions in technology leadership, with the Biden administration initiating a high-stakes investigation into China’s expanding dominance in legacy semiconductor manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Wait is Over: GIMP 3.0 Revolutionizes Image Editing with High-Resolution Interface and Backward Compatibility
item.description: GIMP 3.0, the latest version of popular image editor GNU Image Manipulation Program, promises a major milestone in its history, bringing modern features while maintaining user experience, expected to be released in late December or early January
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: The AI backlash couldn’t have come at a better time
item.description: At a developers conference I attended not too long ago, attendees did little to hide their disdain every time the term “AI” was bandied about.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How digital transformation is reshaping energy systems
item.description: A balanced planet requires more energy with less carbon. The energy transition to a lower carbon future is arguably the largest and most significant challenge ever contemplated or attempted in the history of our planet.
item.dc:creator: Rakesh Jaggi, Fast Company

item.title: DHS OCIO uses a measured approach for IT resilience strategy
item.description: DHS’ OCIO provides two major services. It acts as the CIO for the management zone, providing endpoints, services and delivery to the CXOs. The post DHS OCIO uses a measured approach for IT resilience strategy first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Chat with Santa or find out if you are on the naughty list with these tech features
item.description: You can access the new (and limited!) Santa Chat feature through the ChatGPT app by starting a new chat, hitting the live chat button.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, NewsNation

item.title: Bluesky finds with growth comes growing pains
item.description: Bluesky’s growth trajectory — with a user base that has more than doubled since October — could make it a serious competitor to other social platforms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Who’s who in Donald Trump’s new Silicon Valley entourage
item.description: There’s a larger coterie of tech world Trump supporters who are now working with the incoming administration, with varying degrees of formality.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: AI will eavesdrop on world's wildest places to track and help protect endangered wildlife
item.description: A biologist hid 350 audio monitors across Costa Rica’s tropical rainforests to spy on endangered spider monkeys in order to help protect them.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Future of Remote Tech Support: How SetMe is Revolutionizing the Industry
item.description: Discover how SetMe, a cutting-edge remote desktop software, is transforming the way businesses provide technical support to their employees, no matter where they are in the world.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: More Bugs Uncovered in Windows 11's December Update | Latest Tech News
item.description: Windows 11's December update has been plagued by bugs, including problems with the Start menu and other issues that are affecting users.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The 4 key aspects of a successful data strategy
item.description: How do data strategies work and do companies even need them? This question is on the minds of many business leaders today. At the same time, they are under increasing pressure to remain competitive and innovative.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Albania bans TikTok for a year following death of teenager
item.description: Albania’s prime minister said the country will be banning TikTok for one year, citing violence and bullying among children after a teenager died.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe aims to fly closer to the sun like never before
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A NASA spacecraft aims to fly closer to the sun than any object sent before. The Parker Solar Probe was launched in 2018 to get a close-up look at the sun. Since then, it has flown straight through the sun's corona: the outer atmosphere visible during a total solar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The future belongs to systems of action
item.description: The world of enterprise tech is built on sturdy foundations. For decades, systems of record—the databases, customer relationship management (CRM), and enterprise resource planning (ERP) platforms we’re all intimately familiar with—have served as reliable pillars underpinning every major business.
item.dc:creator: John Sabino, Fast Company

item.title: AI cameras are giving DC's air defense a major upgrade
item.description: Artificial intelligence-based cameras are giving air defense operators unprecedented capabilities in monitoring and protecting airspace.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple facilities. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union continued a strike Friday at seven of the company's delivery hubs just days before Christmas. At midnight on Saturday, workers at a prominent unionized warehouse in New York will also join, the Teamsters said.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nvidia’s Run:ai acquisition waved through by EU
item.description: Nvidia will face no objections to its plan to acquire Israeli AI orchestration software vendor Run:ai Labs in Europe, after the European Commission gave the deal its approval Friday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Scholz dismisses Musk's assertion that only a far-right party can 'save' Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz on Friday dismissed an assertion by Elon Musk that only a far-right party can “save Germany,” but said that freedom of opinion "also goes for multibillionaires.” Germany is expected to vote in an early election on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Google’s contractors told to evaluate Gemini responses outside their expertise
item.description: Behind the responses from genAI models are testers who evaluate those answers for accuracy, but a report released this week casts doubt on the process.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon workers strike at multiple facilities as Teamsters seek labor contract
item.description: Workers at seven Amazon facilities went on strike Thursday, an effort by the Teamsters union to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk considers funding Nigel Farage's populist party, a move that could shake up UK politics
item.description: LONDON (AP) — It’s a photo that sent a tremor through British politics: Elon Musk flanked by British politician Nigel Farage and a wealthy backer, in front of a gilt-framed painting of a young Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Massive data breach at federal credit union exposes 240,000 members
item.description: A credit union with over 240,000 members recently revealed it was targeted by cybercriminals, resulting in a data breach that was part of a two-month attack by hackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple complains Meta requests risk privacy in spat over EU efforts to widen access to iPhone tech
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple complained that requests from Meta Platforms for access to its operating software threaten user privacy, in a spat fueled by the European Union's intensifying efforts to get the iPhone maker to open up to products from tech rivals.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Barcelona subway recycles energy from braking into power to charge electric cars
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — The Barcelona subway grinds to a halt, the doors slide open and commuters pour out to go about their daily business. Little do they know that as they do so, a burst of energy is sent up to street level to help charge an electric car.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, NewsNation
